ground_truth.title: 12 Rules to Arrange Living Room Furniture
ground_truth.author: Lauren Flanagan
ground_truth.main_content: The right furniture layout is key to creating a functional and relaxing space. These tips for arranging furniture will get you there. Arranging living room furniture is a tricky challenge that requires a good eye and a systematic approach. Here are 12 rules to help you create the perfect living room layout. Choose a focal point for your room, whether it's a fireplace, a window with a view, or your television. Arrange your furniture around this focal point to create a natural conversation area. Think about traffic flow and leave clear pathways between furniture pieces. Allow at least 18 inches between the coffee table and sofa for comfortable movement. Create distinct zones within an open floor plan using furniture placement and area rugs to define separate living spaces. Scale your furniture to the size of your room, and avoid blocking natural light sources by placing furniture away from windows when possible.

Scale your furniture to the size of your room, and avoid blocking natural light sources.
